zh.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard De Hoyvil於大約1203年出生在英國哈普爾。她於1223年與Richard De Hoyvil在英國英格蘭哈普爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard De Hoyvil于大约1203年出生在英国哈普爾。她于1223年与Richard De Hoyvil在英国英格兰哈普爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hoyvil
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Havill
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from two places in northern France, Hauville in Eure, and Hauteville la Guichard in La Manche.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard De Hoyvil was born about 1203, in Hampshire, England. She married Richard De Hoyvil in 1223, in Hampshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard De Hoyvil est née environ 1203, à Hampshire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Richard De Hoyvil en 1223, à Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard De Hoyvil wurde um 1203 in Hampshire, England geboren. Sie hat 1223 in Hampshire, England, United Kingdom Richard De Hoyvil geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard De Hoyvil è nata il circa 1203 nel Hampshire, England. Lei sposato Richard De Hoyvil nel 1223, nel Hampshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.Hampshire, England: イギリスイギリスハンプシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard De Hoyvilは約1203年にイギリスハンプシュアイアで生まれた。1223年にイギリスイギリスハンプシュアイアでRichard De Hoyvilと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard De Hoyvil(은)는 대략 1203년 영국 함프실르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1223년 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르에서 Richard De Hoyvil(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard De Hoyvil nasceu aproximadamente 1203, em Hampshire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Richard De Hoyvil em 1223, em Hampshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard De Hoyvil родилась около 1203 г. в местности хампшир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard De Hoyvil в 1223 г. в местности хампшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard De Hoyvil nació aproximadamente 1203, en Hampshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con Richard De Hoyvil en 1223, en Hampshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
